repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
full_text_tesim: Folios are identified by reference to the plates in Brock 1995
Melkite community; transliteration of the litanies in Leiden UB Or. 14236 (ff. 85v-86r) in Syriac letters adapted to the Sogdian and Uyghur writings points to a central Asia provenance

Palimpsest: Armenian Erkatagir. (a) Inner part of the top of a folio, with parts of eight lines; Armenian underwriting; (b) Almost complete folio, quite well preserved; (c) and (d) Two consecutive damaged folios, with Armenian underwriting; (e) Damaged top half of a folio, with Armenian underwriting; (f) Fragment from the middle of a folio with parts of eleven lines, and with traces of Syriac underwriting; (g) Diagonal strip; (h) Small scrap with seven half lines; (i) Folio with damaged top, inner side and bottom, Armenian underwriting.
Four damaged folios and five further fragments
